tiles.title: Avg Store by Date
tiles.insight: The average store sales for most dates are around 23.00, with no clear trend or change over time. This consistency suggests that sales have been relatively stable across different periods.

tiles.title: Store distribution
tiles.insight: Most stores are located around 1.00, with a relatively even spread of other locations. This balanced distribution suggests we can trust the analysis without worrying about a few extreme store locations skewing the results. So what, we can use this data to make generalizations about our retail business without worrying about a few unusual store locations.

tiles.title: Date breakdown
tiles.insight: Sales are spread almost evenly across eight different dates, with no single date accounting for more than a third of total sales. This balanced distribution suggests that sales are consistent across the period shown.

tiles.title: Correlation with Store
tiles.insight: Weekly_Sales is strongly and negatively linked to Store, with stores selling less when they're located together. This suggests that sales are not being driven by the store location itself, but by other factors.

tiles.title: Data completeness
tiles.insight: Every cell in your dataset has a value — nothing is missing. This means all analysis will use the full dataset.
